mnemonics.title: CIA Triad
mnemonics.content: Remember CIA as 'Can't Imagine Anything' else without these three: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability

mnemonics.title: Passive vs Active
mnemonics.content: PASSIVE = Watch (observe without touching). ACTIVE = Attack (modify/damage). P.A.W. = Passive Attacker Waits, Active Attacker Works

mnemonics.title: Active Attack Types
mnemonics.content: MARD: Masquerade, Alter (Modification), Replay, Denial (DoS) - Remember 'MAr RD'

mnemonics.title: Security Services
mnemonics.content: CINA: Confidentiality, Integrity, Non-repudiation, Authentication - 'CINA protects your data'

acronyms.usage: Attack that makes resources unavailable to legitimate users
Entity that assists in key distribution and authentication (e.g., Certificate Authorities)

acronyms.expansion: Network Security Agency / Not Applicable (in context)
acronyms.usage: In network security, refers to security mechanisms

visualTips.visualization: Draw a box with Sender → [Encrypted Message] → Secure Channel → Receiver, with an Adversary trying to intercept from the side. Label: Message Source → Encryption → Transmitted Data → Decryption → Destination
Think of a three-legged stool - if one leg (Availability) breaks, the whole system (security) fails. All three must work together.
Passive = Person sitting in a corner watching (👀). Active = Person running around changing things (✏️). Passive is silent observation; Active is making changes.
Think of an onion - each layer (Authentication, Encryption, Access Control) adds protection. If one is breached, others still protect.

analogies.analogy: Like a locked safe - only those with the key (decryption key) can access the contents (plaintext).
Like a private tunnel underground - no one can see or access what's passing through except the endpoints.
Like someone reading over your shoulder on a train - they observe without touching.
Like someone tampering with your mail - opening, reading, altering, or destroying it.
Like showing ID at a secure building - proving you are who you claim to be.
Like registered mail with signature required - the sender cannot deny sending, and receiver cannot deny receiving.
